2605.19812.title: FLUXtrapolation: A benchmark on extrapolating ecosystem fluxes
2605.19812.paper_summary: We introduce FLUXtrapolation, a benchmark for extrapolating ecosystem fluxes under progressively harder distribution shifts. Ecosystem fluxes are central to understanding the carbon, water, and energy cycles, yet they can only be measured directly at sparsely located measurement towers. Producing global flux estimates therefore requires training models on observed sites using globally available covariates and predicting in unobserved regions, that is, upscaling. Flux upscaling is a challenging domain generalization problem that is affected by a shift in covariate distribution across climates, ecosystem types, and environmental conditions, as well as by conditional shift: important drivers remain unobserved at global scale. We provide a quantitative analysis of both these shifts in $P_X$ and $P_{Y\mid X}$. FLUXtrapolation is designed based on domain expertise on flux upscaling: it defines temporal, spatial, and temperature-based extrapolation scenarios and evaluates performance across held-out domains, temporal aggregations, and tail errors. In a pilot study, we find that baselines perform similarly under median hourly RMSE, but separate under the proposed tail-focused and multi-scale evaluation. FLUXtrapolation therefore poses a realistic and thus relevant challenge for machine learning methods under distribution shift; at the same time, progress on this benchmark would directly support the scientific goal of improving flux upscaling.
2605.19812.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹FLUXtrapolation，一個用於在逐漸更困難的分佈變化下外推生態系統通量的基準。生態系統通量對於理解碳、水和能量循環至關重要，但它們只能在稀疏分佈的測量塔上直接測量。因此，產生全球通量估計需要在觀測站點上訓練模型，使用全球可用的協變量，並在未觀測的區域進行預測，即上行擴展。通量上行擴展是一個具有挑戰性的領域泛化問題，受到氣候、生態系統類型和環境條件下協變量分佈變化的影響，以及條件變化：重要的驅動因素在全球範圍內仍然未被觀測。我們對$P_X$和$P_{Y\mid X}$這兩種變化提供了定量分析。FLUXtrapolation是基於通量上行擴展的領域專業知識設計的：它定義了基於時間、空間和溫度的外推情境，並評估在保留的領域、時間聚合和尾部誤差中的表現。在一項初步研究中，我們發現基準模型在中位數每小時RMSE下表現相似，但在提出的尾部聚焦和多尺度評估下則表現分開。因此，FLUXtrapolation對於在分佈變化下的機器學習方法提出了一個現實且相關的挑戰；同時，對這一基準的進展將直接支持改善通量上行擴展的科學目標。
2605.19812.author: Anya Fries et.al.
2605.19812.authors: Anya Fries, Jacob A Nelson, Martin Jung, Markus Reichstein, Jonas Peters
